title: Reverse Nodes in k-Group
pattern: Linked List â€” Recursive/Iterative Group Reversal
coreIdea: Reverse every k consecutive nodes in a linked list. Check if k nodes exist ahead first, then reverse that group using standard pointer reversal, and recursively/iteratively connect to the reversed next group.
recognitionCues: asks to reverse in fixed-size groups, not the whole list
leftover nodes fewer than k typically left unreversed
approach: Check if k nodes remain from current position; if not, return head as-is. Otherwise, reverse the next k nodes using standard iterative reversal, then recursively call the function on the remaining list and attach it to the tail of the just-reversed group (which was the original head).
spaceComplexity: O(n/k) recursion stack, O(1) if done iteratively
